observations.recommendation: Management shall come up with specific guidelines to be enacted by the Sangguniang Panlungsod relative to the operations of the City Pasalubong Center to include among others, the manner of granting seed money for its operating capital, accounting for inventory, income and expenses, procurement, hiring and compensating employees who will man the business. Considering that, unlike other public utility and economic enterprise enumerated above which is a service business, the Pasalubong Center has the nature of a trading and merchandising business of the City Government, hence it should be registered with the BIR for the issuance of separate official receipts to its customers and clienteles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant prepare and submit the complete set of financial statements on time for all funds consisting of: (a) Pre- and Post-closing Trial Balance; (b) Adjusting and closing Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs); (c) Detailed and Condensed Statement of Financial Position; (d) Detailed and Condensed Statement of Financial Performance; (e); Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity; (f) Statement of Cash Flows; (g) Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts; (h) Notes to Financial Statements; (i) Statement of Management Responsibility for Financial Statements; (j) Subsidiary Schedule of General Ledger (GL) Accounts, to allow the timely audit thereof and the timely submission of the required report.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management take the following immediate courses of actions:
Require the City Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to record in the books all the reconciling items, after a thorough analysis thereof, and collating the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the same, for a fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements;
Direct the Acting City Treasurer to submit immediately to the City Accounting Office copies of the bank debit and credit memoranda, to ensure immediate recording thereof in the books of accounts.
We also recommended that Management require the concerned accountable officers at the City Public Market to remit the unremitted collections in CY 2016.  Continue with the investigation that was initiated in CY 2016 and impose criminal and / or administrative sanctions, if warranted.
We recommended that Management ensure that the Liquidating Officer be appropriately bonded in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended that the Petty Cash Custodian to rule and foot the cashbook, accomplish the required certification at the end of each month, and indicate the appropriate details or nature of transactions in the Cashbook as prescribed under the NGAS.

We recommended that the Management revisit the sufficiency of the established petty cash fund and direct the Petty Cash Fund Custodian to replenish as soon as the disbursements reaches 75 per cent or as needed, to allow sufficient time between submitting the replenishment and receiving the fund.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the:
Inventory Committee/Team to conduct a thorough and complete physical inventory of the City’s properties, particularly Land Improvements and Infrastructure Assets.  Thereafter, prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed form (Annex 49, NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II) and submit the same to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and
City Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, in accordance with the forms prescribed in Annexes 9 and 37 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II, respectively and conduct periodic reconciliation of the accounting and property records; and
We also recommended that Management conduct of the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, so that only serviceable and existing items at year-end remain as PPE in the books to allow fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the year-end financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the City Accounting Office to:
Reconcile their records with the City Engineering Office to verify the status of the infrastructure projects;
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the completed CIP into appropriate PPE accounts and to take up in the books the cost of progress works; and
Strictly comply with the accrual basis of accounting in preparing the financial statements in accordance with the IPSAS.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to:
Reclassify the amount as Loans Payable-Domestic account for a more accurate presentation of the liabilities of the City in its year-end Statement of Financial Position; and
Coordinate with the NHA for the processing and transmittal of the turnover documents for the said housing project as supporting documents to its recorded Other Structure account.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to review thoroughly the long-outstanding and undocumented payables and prepare appropriate adjusting entries for the reversion to surplus and/or to re-establish the valid claims. In case of the amount recorded in the Trust Fund books, determine whether the project has been completed and coordinate with the source agencies for the appropriate disposition of the remaining funds.

We recommended that Management require the City Accounting Office to revisit whether the income accounts, amounting to ₱1,934,396.24, entails obligations from the LGU, otherwise, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to take up these receipts as revenues. Henceforth, strictly comply with the pertinent accounting standards in the recording of financial transactions.

We recommended that Management:
Require that the City Accounting Office comply with the prescribed accounting standards in recording the income from PhilHealth payments and expenses of the City’s health centers and lying-in clinic;
Require the coordination of City Accounting Office and each HCI for the recording and tracking of claims filed to the PhilHealth; and
Require that the City Health Board efficiently utilize the health funds for the improvement of health care services of the City of Tabaco.

We recommended that Management:
Strengthen the monitoring of project implementation and verify its completeness in accordance with the approved plans and specification;
Coordinate with the barangay officials for the maintenance and sustainability of the projects; and
Implement necessary remedial actions to make the projects operational and maximized the economic benefits therefrom.

We recommended that Management instruct the:
City Accountant to record in the books the remaining balance of donated COVID-19 vaccines pursuant to the requirements of RA No. 11525 and COA Circular No. 2020-009; and
City Health Office to submit the PTRs and duly accomplished supporting documents to the GSO and Office of the City Accountant to facilitate evaluation and recording of receipts and issuances/utilization of COVID-19 vaccines in the books and property records.

We recommended that Management:
Enforce the submission of liquidation reports by the IA in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013;
Monitor the IA’s utilization of fund transfers to ensure the timely and proper implementation of the approved projects; and
Further, ensure that funds previously transferred to the IA have been liquidated before additional funds are released.

We recommended that Management require the City Accounting Office to:
Inquire in writing from the Source Agencies of the idle accounts, regarding the proper disposition of the balance of fund transfers in the absence of copies of the pertinent MOAs; and
To exert effort in tracing the unidentified balances and effect necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct account balances in the year-end financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Acting City Treasurer to:
Prepare a systematic, comprehensive and realistic revenue forecasting system taking into account prior years’ collections and the tax information system of the City;
Prepare periodic reports to monitor the status of revenue generation and to serve as guide on the appropriate measures to be adopted in case of shortfall in the projected revenue targets; and
Adhere to forecasting guidelines set by BLGF and prepare a counter forecast in case the City disagrees with the BLGF local revenue targets.
In addition, we recommended that the LCE request the Sangguniang Panlungsod to commence its public hearing in relation to the revision of real property assessment as soon as the moratorium lapses.

We recommended that Management:
Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF through the timely implementation of the identified DRRM related PPAs;
Monitor the implementation of PPAs to facilitate its prompt completion and timely reprogramming of unexpended balances; and
Ensure that the stockpile inventory charged under the LDRRMF be used for its intended purpose that is related to disaster response and recovery operations.

We recommended that the Local School Board coordinate with the concerned City personnel in determining if there are available additional funds for the SEF Budget for the current year. Also, appropriate the same so as to provide additional fund in consonance with the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that Management:
Exert effort to ensure that the Special Education Fund of the City is efficiently utilized and that the implementation of all identified PPAs be given priority to warrant the full attainment of goals in accordance with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1 No. 1, series of 2017; and
Monitor regularly the progress of each PPAs implementation to achieve the desired level of efficiency.

We commended Management for its untiring effort in the protection of the environment and public health.  We likewise recommended that Management through its Solid Waste Management Board continue implementing its 10-year plan properly and to assess its effectiveness and relevance in relation to developments in the field of solid waste management.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System, in coordination with the City Planning and Developement Office, create a GAD database to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data as basis in GAD planning, programming and policy making.
We also recommended that the LCE direct all members of the GAD Focal Point System to undergo trainings on HGDG, PIMME and FIMME to build their competence in the conduct of gender analysis of programs and projects for purposes of GAD attribution.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Person to:
Adhere to the approved GPB in the implemention of GAD programs and activities.  In case of changes, Management should prepare a revised GPB for approval by the concerned officials and oversight agencies; and
Prepare the GAD AR accurately and submit copies thereof to the oversight bodies, including the complete accompanying reports such as: 1) brief summary of the reported program or project; (2) results of HGDG tests; and (3) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor for verification.

We recommended that:
Management require the CHRMO to prepare and issue a staffing pattern plan and policy guidelines in the hiring of employees under job order and contract of service; and
CHRMO properly assess the manpower needs and staffing requirement per office or department and establish a reasonable number of plantilla positions for the regularization of qualified and competent contractual and job order employees, if warranted, in order to avoid the hiring of excessive job order employees and the duplication of work performed by permanent employees and job order personnel.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly adheres to the documentary requirements on the release of the salaries and wages of job order; and
Direct the Disbursing Officer to see to it that each claimant in the payroll properly sign therein and not to allow the release of the salaries and wages of job order personnel to a person other than the payee without the authorization from the claimant and the presentation of identification documents of the person claiming in behalf of the payee.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require that the CHRMO (i) impose the appropriate administrative disciplinary action to the JOs concerned as well as to their immediate supervisor or head of office who allowed such act and require the JOs concerned to reflect the proper time entries in their DTRs; (ii) impose strict compliance with the job order contracts executed by the City Government; and
Initiate the necessary investigation and impose administrative sanctions, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to cause the refund of the excess withholdings to the appropriate suppliers and/or contractor of goods and services and to remit in full the taxes withheld to the BIR within the period prescribed under Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
City Accountant to conduct the necessary analysis and reconciliation to properly ascertain the correct amount of the Due to GSIS account; and
Acting City Treasurer to remit the full amount withheld from the employee’s salaries together with the government’s share in the employees’ premiums within the period prescribed by RA No. 8291.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
City Accountant to cause the necessary analysis and reconciliation in order to properly ascertain the value of the Due to PhilHealth account; and
City Treasurer to remit the full amount withheld from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share in the employees’ premiums.

We recommended that the City Local Chief Executive direct the:
City Accountant to cause the necessary analysis and reconciliation to properly ascertain the correct value of the Due to Pag-IBIG account; and
Acting City Treasurer to remit the full amount withheld from the employee’s salaries together with the government’s share in the employees’ premiums.

We recommended that the City Treasurer orient the AOs assigned on the CSWDO on the proper maintenance of their cashbook and accompanying records pursuant to Section 28 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units, Volume II.

We recommended that Management require the Accountable Officers to manually prepare and submit the monthly individual and consolidated RAAFs not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month in accordance with the NGAS and ensure the propriety of the reports.

We recommended that Management ensure the proper turnover of accountabilities between the outgoing and incoming accountable officers, issuance of clearance to outgoing AOs and adherence to the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 in order to determine correctly their accountabilities.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management take the following immediate courses of actions: (a) Require the City Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to record in the books all the reconciling items, after a thorough analysis thereof, and collating the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the same, for a fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements; and (b) Direct the Acting City Treasurer to submit immediately to the City Accounting Office copies of the bank debit and credit memoranda, to ensure immediate recording thereof in the books of accounts.

We also recommended that Management require the concerned accountable officers at the City Public Market to remit the unremitted collections in CY 2016.  Continue with the investigation that was initiated in CY 2016 and initiate appropriate legal action therefor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer orient the AOs assigned on the CSWDO on the proper maintenance of their cashbook and accompanying records pursuant to Section 28 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units, Volume II.

We recommended that management require the Accountable Officers to manually prepare and submit the monthly individual and consolidated RAAFs not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month in accordance with the NGAS and ensure the propriety of the reports.

We recommended that Management ensure the proper turnover of accountabilities between the outgoing and incoming accountable officers, issuance of clearance to outgoing AOs and adherence to the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 in order to determine correctly their accountabilities.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management take the following immediate courses of actions:
Require the City Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to record in the books all the reconciling items, after a thorough analysis thereof, and collating the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the same, for a fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements;
Direct the Acting City Treasurer to submit immediately to the City Accounting Office copies of the bank debit and credit memoranda, to ensure immediate recording thereof in the books of accounts.
We also recommended that Management require the concerned accountable officers at the City Public Market to remit the unremitted collections in CY 2016.  Continue with the investigation that was initiated in CY 2016 and initiate appropriate legal action therefor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the concerned personnel/department to implement internal controls over the management and reporting of inventories and PPE by observing the prescribed procedures in the receiving, issuance and recording thereof and use of the prescribed records and reports;
Require the City Accountant to record the monthly receipt and issuance of inventories and properties as part of the monthly closing of books; and
Ensure the conduct of physical count of inventories and PPE, as well as the one-time cleansing of PPE and reconcile their records with the Accounting.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that: (a) delivery of all supplies / inventory items be accepted by Property Inspector who is a personnel of the GSO; (b) issuances of supplies / inventory items be centralized at the General Services Office; and (c) the GSO be required to keep and maintain records pertaining to receipts, issuances of supplies / inventory items;
Require the OIC-General Services Officer to initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all Supplies of the City Government at year-end and prepare and submit the corresponding Reports of Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) to the Audit Team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balances in the financial statements; and
The City Accountant and OIC-General Service Officer maintain a complete and updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly conduct reconciliation thereof so that any discrepancies noted could be promptly reconciled and adjusted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
City Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to take up the issuance of semi-expendable properties; and
OIC-General Services Officer to use the prescribed form of Inventory Custodian Slip and prepare a list of these assets for monitoring.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the:
Inventory Committee/Team to conduct a thorough and complete physical inventory of the City’s properties, particularly Land Improvements, Infrastructure Assets and Building & Other Structures.  Thereafter, prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed form (Annex 49, NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II) and submit the same to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and
City Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, in accordance with the forms prescribed in Annexes 9 and 37 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II, respectively and conduct periodic reconciliation of the accounting and property records.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in implementing projects funded by other agencies to ensure that funds are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner and that services and benefits due to the public are not hampered.

We recommended that Management instruct the CDRRMO to update the DRR related data and statistics and revisit the plans, programs and activities in the LDRRMP to ensure a holistic, comprehensive, integrated and proactive approach against the identified disasters in the City. Likewise, we recommended to prepare the annual LDRRMP/LDRRMFIP which shall include the details of the priority projects and activities for the unexpended balance of LDRRMF from previous years.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Require the CDRRMO to plan/identify PPAs to utilize the various cash donations according to its specific purpose and to address the needs of the intended beneficiaries;
Require the CDRRMO, together with the Local Development Council to incorporate in the AIP and LDRRMP/LDRRMFIP all PPAs to be implemented for the year; and
Require the City Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to (i) revert to the General Fund the unutilized LDRRMF aged more than five years, to be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian; and (ii) the transfer of assets to the General Fund Books and recognition of expense for the issued supplies and equipment in the Trust Fund Books.

We recommended that the Local School Board coordinate with the City personnel in determining if there are available additional funds for the SEF Budget for the current year. Also, appropriate the same so as to provide additional fund in consonance with the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that Management through the Solid Waste Management Board continue with the proper implementation of its 10-year plan and to assess its effectiveness and relevance in relation to developments in the field of solid waste management.

We recommended that Management revisit its GAD PPAs, providing more attention and study on how to maximize the utilization of the GAD in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management:
through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender responsive.
use the HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying PPAs attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring of its effectiveness.

We recommended that the OIC-City Engineer submit a satisfactory explanation and/or justification on the exceptions/discrepancies noted in the Technical Evaluation Report for further evaluation, without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance if warranted.
Henceforth, we recommended that the City Government of Tabaco strictly observe the guidelines in the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract to obtain bid prices that will be most advantageous to the government.

We recommended that the LCE revisit the provisions as provided under Section 13 of the Annex “E” of the RA No. 9184 and its IRR on the evaluation of contractor’s performance and constitute the mandated Constructors Performance Evaluation System – Implementing Unit (CPES-IU) in the City of Tabaco.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to:
Adhere to the amendments to the Tax Code under RA No. 11534, otherwise known as “Corporate Recovery and Tax Incentives for Enterprises Act”
Refund the excess withholdings of taxes to the appropriate Suppliers/Contractor of Goods and Services; and
Remit in full the taxes withheld to the BIR within the time prescribed by BIR rules and regulations to avoid the payment of penalties incident to delayed remittance.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the LSB to formulate/prepare the required three-year strategic program and the recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly the are bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the next three-year strategic program for the SEF Annual Budget.

Henceforth, the LSB should strictly follow the required processes and documentation in the planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned Joint Circular.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to immediately reconcile the Cash - Local Treasury and Cash-in Bank-Local Currency, Current Accounts, and record the necessary adjustments.  Further, require all accountable officers to update their cashbooks daily.
Henceforth, the City Treasurer and the OIC-City Accountant to reconcile their books of accounts, at least quarterly, to avoid incurrence of accumulated unreconciled balances.

We recommended that Management:
Require the OIC-City Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs)  to record in the books all the reconciling items, after a thorough analysis thereof, and collating the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the same, for a fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements; and
Direct the City Treasurer to submit immediately to the City Accounting Office copies of the bank debit and credit memoranda, to ensure immediate recording thereof in the books of accounts.
We recommended that Management continue to safeguard from wastage the government funds by adhering to COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004.

We recommended that the OIC-City Accountant make a detailed analysis on the RPT/SET Receivable accounts and its related Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts and make the necessary adjustments to ensure the fair presentation of the accounts in the yearend financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the City Treasurer to update the RPTAR /Taxpayer’s index card based on the Report of RPT Collectibles for CY 2020 which was also based on the Assessment Roll as of December, 2019 and reconcile with the certified  list given to the City Accounting Office and furnish the latter with the correct certified list of taxes due and collectible as basis in establishing the correct RPT/SET Receivables and the related Deferred RPT/Deferred SET Income accounts.
Also we recommended that the OIC-City Accountant review/reconcile the corrected certified list and report with the amount set up as RPT and SET receivable accounts for CY 2020, to reflect the correct amounts of receivable accounts.

We recommended that Management observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in implementing projects funded by other agencies to ensure that funds are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner and that services and benefits due to the public are not hampered.

We recommended that:
The OIC-City Accountant and OIC-City General Services Officer reconcile their records on Inventories and determine the cause/s of the discrepancy.  Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments in their respective records, as needed;
Henceforth, both officials maintain complete and updated subsidiary records on Inventories and conduct regular reconciliation thereof, at least on a quarterly basis, to ensure accuracy of the Inventory accounts; and
The OIC-General Services Officer conduct a complete physical inventory-taking of all Supplies of the City Government at year-end and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI to the Audit Team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management follow the prescribed procedures in the receipt, custody, issuance and recording for all types of inventory as prescribed under Section 490 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 118 and 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I.

We recommended that Management require the:
OIC-City Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to reconcile their records  of the PPE accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereto;
Further, for both officials to maintain complete PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, in accordance with the forms prescribed in Annexes 9 and 37 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II. Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of their respective records, at least on a quarterly basis, to ensure accuracy of the PPE accounts; and
Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the City’s properties. Thereafter, prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed form and submit the same to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that Management:
Designate specific personnel to work on the transfer of the titles of the 121 lots in the name of the City and appropriate funds to cover its cost;
Require the OIC-City Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to maintain Real Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for each land owned by the City.  Further, for both officials  to reconcile their subsidiary records regularly to ensure correctness of the accounts; and
Require the Inventory Team to prepare a complete inventory report of PPEs, including the real properties, and reconcile the report with the GSO records. Thereafter, furnish the Audit Team a copy of the report.

We recommended that:
The General Service Officer keep a complete and updated LRNPCs to serve as a means of checking the accuracy of the LRNLCs maintained by the City Accountant;
Both officials conduct regular reconciliation of the balances of their records, at least on a quarterly basis, to ensure that their records are consistent with each other; and
The City Engineer furnish the City Accountant and the General Services Officer complete description and cost segregation of road components for completed road projects as basis for recording in their respective subsidiary records.

We recommended that Management require the OIC-City Accountant to revert the Accounts Payable which have been outstanding for two years or more to the Unappropriated Surplus account and appropriated in the next year’s budget.
Henceforth, we recommended that Management continue to abide with the reporting requirements on all COVID-19 funds received.

We also recommended that compliance to posting requirements and other pertinent regulations be maintained to continuously uphold transparency and accountability in the procurement transactions of the City.

We recommended that Management:
Submit to the Audit Team the appropriate payrolls and other lacking supporting documents (Annex 7) for the Social Amelioration Program-Emergency Subsidy Program (SAP-ESP);
Require the OIC-City Accountant to ensure that disbursements of funds are properly supported with the complete required supporting documents; and
In future subsidies, strictly comply with the terms and conditions stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement to ensure that the subsidies are actually received by the targeted beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management direct the GSO to coordinate with the CSWDO and prepare the following reports and submit to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team, within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-009:
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-kind Donations;
Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; and
Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of In-kind Donations

We recommended that Management submit the above-cited lacking documents and henceforth, adopt policies on the grant of financial assistance to its constituents to ensure that the same are granted to qualified beneficiaries only.

We recommended that Management submit authority from the Source Agencies or Entities for the use of the subject fund to COVID-19 response and recommendation of the CDRRMC for the same.  Henceforth, ensure that trust funds received by the City be spent strictly in accordance with the purposes for which these were received.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The City Treasurer and the City Accountant to submit to the LSB the certified statement containing the following reports, for the latter to properly compute the amount available for appropriation, namely: (i) actual SEF Income for the immediately preceding fiscal year; (ii) actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year; (iii) the estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year; and (iv) the annual estimates for the ensuing year; and
The LSB the early submission of the complete SIPs by the school heads, indicating therein the priority needs of the schools based on DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017, together with budget matrix showing its sources, excluding donation/solicitation, in order that the same may be considered in the preparation of the SEF Budget.
We likewise reiterate our prior year’s recommendations that the LSB:
Strictly adhere to the prescribed formula in the computation of the available appropriation for SEF; and
Formulate/prepare the required three-year strategic program and SIPs which are necessary input in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and also furnish the Audit Team of the same for reference and review purposes.

We recommended that the LSB furnish the concerned offices, particularly the City Accounting and the Budget Offices, for recording in the books any resolutions made regarding the changes in the SEF budget.
Likewise, the City Accounting Office to record the necessary information in their subsidiary ledger to serve as reference in the verification of balances and as a means of checking the accuracy of the records maintained by the Budget Office.

We recommended that the LCE require the City Accountant to make the necessary correcting entry charging the purchased 47 refrigerators from SEF to General Fund and furnish the Audit Team a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for verification.
Henceforth, require the City Budget Office and the City Accounting Office to refrain from processing the payment of those expenses that are not properly chargeable against the SEF.

We commended Management for the improved efficiency in the utilization of the GAD budget.  We also recommended, however, that Management observe the required allocation of at least five per cent of its Annual Budget to Gender and Development in order that more funds will be available for the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities.
Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person, the City Accounting and City Budget Offices to coordinate with each other in the preparation of accurate financial reports on GAD.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender responsive.
Likewise, use the HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying PPAs attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring of its effectiveness.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-City Accountant to:

Remit in full the taxes withheld to the BIR within the time prescribed by BIR rules and regulations to avoid the payment of penalties incident to delayed remittance.

City Treasurer to remit the full amount that was withheld from employees’ salary, together with the government’s share in the employees premiums; and
Appropriate Office in charge with updating of employees’ names, their corresponding employment status, positions, salaries, and such other pertinent information, including subsequent changes therein, to regularly submit the Agency Remittance Advice (ARA) to the GSIS.

City Treasurer to remit the full amount that was withheld from employees’ salary, together with the government’s share in the employees’ premiums.

We recommended, however, that the City Treasurer continue coordinating with the City Accountant for the complete and immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the cash accounts in the GL and Cashbooks, respectively, and make the necessary adjustments thereon. Henceforth, in compliance with Section 189 of the GAAM, the City Treasurer and the City Accountant shall reconcile their books of accounts, at least quarterly, to avoid incurrence of accumulated unreconciled balances.

Further, we recommended that the City Treasurer/Accountable Officer regularly maintain and update complete cashbook per bank account and likewise maintain duly certified printed copies of the monthly updated and reconciled cashbooks.

Furthermore, we recommended that the CAO record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the books to fairly present the Cash accounts in the financial statements

Direct the GSO and the Accountant to continue updating its stock/property cards and ledger to facilitate the reconciliation of property records and to immediately address any discrepancy found.

Designate a personnel/team which shall be responsible for securing the land titles of the 121 lots and likewise appropriate funds to cover its cost.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management apply feasible strategies to enforce the collection of rent on a yearly basis from relatives of occupied cemetery lots for more than five years.

We recommended the following courses of actions to correct the noted deficiencies:

The City Treasurer initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all Supplies of the City Government at year-end and prepare and submit the corresponding Report of Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) to the Audit Team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balances in the financial statements; and

The City Accountant and the City Treasurer maintain a complete and updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly conduct reconciliation thereof so that any discrepancies noted could be promptly adjusted and reconciled.
We recommended that Management continue to faithfully take care that government funds are safeguarded from wastage by adhering to COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004.

We recommended, however, that the City Treasurer continue coordinating with the City Accountant for the complete and immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the cash accounts in the GL and Cashbooks, respectively, and make the necessary adjustments thereon. Henceforth, in compliance with Section 189 of the GAAM, the City Treasurer and the City Accountant shall reconcile their books of accounts, at least quarterly, to avoid incurrence of accumulated unreconciled balances.
Further, we recommended that the City Treasurer/Accountable Officer regularly maintain and update complete cashbook per bank account and likewise maintain duly certified printed copies of the monthly updated and reconciled cashbooks.
Furthermore, we recommended that the CAO record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the books to fairly present the Cash accounts in the financial statements as of the yearend.

We reiterated our audit recommendations of prior years that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Direct the GSO and the Accountant to continue updating its stock/property cards and ledger to facilitate the reconciliation of property records and to immediately address any discrepancy found; and
Designate a personnel/team which shall be responsible for securing the land titles of the 121 lots and likewise appropriate funds to cover its cost.

We recommended that the City Accountant take up the necessary adjustments pertaining to the above reconciling items in order to arrive at a fair presentation of the TL-DRRM account as of December 31 2019.

We recommended that the LCE direct:
The Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the RPCLNR to be accomplished in accordance with the Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008;
The CAO and the GSO to prepare and maintain the required LRNLCs and LRNPCs for each local road owned by the LGU and thereafter, conduct regular reconciliation of the records; and
The Engineering Office to provide the CAO and the GSO with complete data relating to the description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Accountant to expedite the submission of the Year-end Trial Balance and financial statements. Henceforth, prepare and submit Monthly Trial Balances and Quarterly Financial Statements with complete schedules and annexes within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with the abovementioned existing rules and regulations;
Take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as augmenting the Accountant’s Office of personnel from other Departments, especially during report preparations and/or allowing them to render overtime, subject to the availability of funds, in order to comply with the timely preparation and submission of these mandatory financial reports, pursuant to mandated deadlines; and
Allow the Accountant to recommend qualified plantilla personnel of the CAO to attend Trainings or Seminars relative to their works, to equip them with the required knowledge and skills in order to continue improving their performance.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accountant to submit immediately to the Auditor the ORs and DVs for the months of April to December 2019 and the accounts for the months of July, and October to December 2019, including the corresponding Financial Reports.  Henceforth, strictly comply with the timelines on the recording of accounts and submission of accounts and reports which is within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month as required by existing laws, rules and regulations.
We also recommended that Management look into the possibility of assigning additional manpower to the CAO and allowing them to render overtime service, subject to availability of funds, in order to enable the Accountant to update the recording of transactions in the books, timely prepare and submit the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials and employees to expedite the completion and submission of documentary requirements of the contracts as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001. Further, compel submission of the As-Built Plans by the contractors before final payment and acceptance of projects.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management reinforce the involvement and participation of barangays, through massive information dissemination of the City’s SWM programs, especially on the proper disposal of wastes and remind the constituents that SWM is not only the responsibility of the City officials and employees but of every individual in the community.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the LSB and concerned officials also give attention and priority to:
a.  Operation and maintenance of public schools; and
b.  Construction and repair of school building, facilities and equipment
We also recommended strict adherence by the LSB to the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 in the utilization of the SEF and the judicious identification of priority program and projects for the coming year.
Further, we recommended that the School Division Superintendent of DepEd- Tabaco City to require the concerned officials to include Sports Development in the budget of their respective district schools and make representations with the DepEd National Office for the inclusion of such budget provision in the annual appropriations of the agency in the General Appropriations Act.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the LSB to formulate/prepare the required three-year strategic program and the recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly the are bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the next three-year strategic program for the SEF Annual Budget.
Henceforth, the LSB should strictly follow the required processes and documentation in the planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned Joint Circular.

We recommended that Management to require strict adherence by DOs to the guidelines on the liquidation of cash advances. Further, as much as possible, to refrain from granting cash advances to non-employees of the City due to lack of direct supervision and to avoid delayed liquidation thereof.

We recommended that the LSB  prepare and submit the required quarterly and annual reports to the oversight agency in the prescribed form and within the prescribed period and observe the posting of Utilization Report in the LGU’s website to enhance\effective monitoring of the  utilization of the SEF thereby promoting transparency and accountability.
Further, the LSB should exercise caution and consistency in the preparation of the LSB Budget and other reporting requirements to ensure that the outputs prepared are correct and proper.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that the LCE direct:
The City Treasurer and OIC-Economic Enterprise Management Unit to strictly enforce collection of the unpaid rentals.  Further, the City strictly impose the provisions of the pertinent City Ordinance and the Contracts of Lease such as imposition of penalties/surcharges to defaulting lessees;
The CTO formulate effective and practical strategies that could enhance collections of unpaid accounts; and
The CTO to submit to the Market Committee and City Legal Office the monthly Status of Delinquent Stallholders, to include necessary, to facilitate prompt and appropriate action; and
The City Legal Office to furnish the CTO the executed compromise agreement and to conduct evaluation/investigation of delinquent stallholders for possible filing of cases.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender-responsive.  Likewise, use HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying programs and projects attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring its effectiveness.
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that Management allocate at least five per cent of the total budget for the implementation of GAD-related activities.

We recommended that Management revisit its GAD PPAs, providing more attention and study on how to maximize the utilization of the GAD in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided.

We commended the City for its compliance on the submission of GAD Plan and Budget as well as the Accomplishment report to the DILG.  We also recommended, however, that the GAD Focal Point System/Focal Person prepare and submit the other required reports to be attached to the GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that Management observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in implementing projects funded by other agencies so as to ensure that funds are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner and that the services and benefits due to the public are not hampered.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management maximize the utilization of its funds and ensure the implementation of PPAs designed to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters, such as those which pertains to disaster prevention/mitigation and disaster preparedness.

We reiterated the following prior year’s recommendation that Management:
Observe proper timing in the implementation of the infrastructure projects to give ample time for the implementing office in the prompt accomplishment of the projects.
Closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% DF that are envisioned to help in the realization of social-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-01 dated 22 February 2017.
Ensure the re-appropriation for eligible development projects of the prior years’ savings of completed projects under the 20% DF to further augment the funds available for the implementation of development projects in the City.

We recommended and also re-iterated paraphrased audit recommendations of the prior years that the Local Chief Executive:

Require the Inventory Team to prepare a complete inventory report of PPEs, including the real properties, and have it reconciled with the GSO records. Thereafter, furnish the Audit Team a copy of the report;

Direct the GSO and the Accountant to continue updating its stock/property cards and ledger cards especially those acquired before 2017 to facilitate the reconciliation of property records and to immediately address any discrepancy found; and

Instruct the concerned personnel to work on securing the land titles of the remaining 124 lots and appropriate funds to cover its cost.

Further, we recommended that the Inventory Committee, offices of the GSO and Accounting shall exert extra effort to locate documents that could shed light to the unreconciled balances between records and investigate causes/reasons for missing or unaccounted properties, including those not found on site and identify the persons deemed liable thereof.

Furthermore, we recommended that Management compel accountable officers/employees to submit required documents needed in the evaluation of request for relief from accountability for losses due to fortuitous events or natural calamities, or due to acts of man, i.e. theft, robbery, arson, etc.

We commended the City for its initial compliance on the Post Closure and Rehabilitation of Closed Dumpsite Project at Barangay San Antonio, Tabaco City. 
We however recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials and employees to expedite the implementation of required activities in accordance with the EMB recommendations and guidelines set in R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender-responsive.  Likewise, use HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying programs and projects attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring its effectiveness.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management consider judicious use, the efficient and effective actions in the disbursement of government fund in addressing gender issues towards the realization of the objectives of City’s commitments, plans and policies on women empowerment and gender equality.

Further, we recommended strict compliance with Section 3.2 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 02 dated September 23, 2016.

Furthermore, we recommended that Management remind the concerned officials and employees on the guidelines for the grant of cash advances that only duly appointed or designated disbursing officers may perform disbursing functions.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines on the preparation of the GAD Accomplishment Report provided in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars 2016-01 and 2013-01 and on the on the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report to COA Audit Team as prescribed in Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that Management revisit their programs/projects, giving more attention and study on how to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs and projects in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were integrated.

We also reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that Management allocate at least five per cent of the total budget for the implementation of GAD-related activities as required under Section 30 of the General Appropriations Act and Section 36 (a) of R.A. 9710 (Magna Carta of Women).

We recommended that Management facilitate the full implementation of the BUB and LGSF-ADM programs/projects/activities in order not to delay the benefits these could provide the intended beneficiaries.

We reiterated prior year’s recommendation that the City Government, through the CDRRMO ensure efficient and effective execution of projects designed to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters, such as those projects which pertain to disaster prevention/mitigation and disaster preparedness.

observations.recommendation: We commended Management for extending a 24-hour ambulance service to its constituents and the general public.  We recommended that Management continue its aim in upholding an efficient and effective delivery of health services and making health one of its priority programs.

We recommended that Management meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Plan. 

Further, Management to observe proper timing in the implementation of the infrastructure projects to give ample time for the implementing office in the prompt accomplishment of the projects.

Furthermore, we recommended that Management closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% DF that are envisioned to help in the realization of social-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-01 dated 22 February 2017.

We also recommended that Management ensure the re-appropraition for eligible development projects of the prior years’ savings from the implementation of projects under the 20% DF for its optimum utilization and attainment of desirable developments in the City.
observations.reason: Deficiency still noted in the audit of CY 2019 accounts. Audit observation and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to: (a) strictly monitor and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivable and payable accounts; (b) prepare necessary adjusting entries for transactions affecting the accounts; (c) prepare detailed aging of receivables and payables once valid supporting documents are retrieved; (d) request for write-off of uncollectible receivables from the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by documents required by the Circular; and (e) issue demand letters or bill of accounts to those identified debtors.

We recommended that Management:

Require City Treasurer furnish the City Accountant, on a periodic basis the necessary data/reports/documents regarding market stall rentals for recording purposes; and

Require the City Accountant that the total realizable income from market stall rental fees is properly taken up in the books of the City Government.

We recommended that the LCE require the CSWD Officer to update regularly their records more particularly those data needed in the preparation of payroll for the Longevity Gift and the Social Pension. 

The Barangay Social Workers and the BASCA Presidents shall be asked to pool resources and be responsible for updating the records/reports relative to the status of the senior citizens of their barangay and regularly submit report to the CSWD.

Pay-out of quarterly social pension shall be on the first month of every quarter in compliance with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 17 Series of 2015

We also recommended vigilance by the Accounting Unit in processing cash advances to guarantee completeness and properly supported transactions to minimize occurrence of recurrent excess in the cash advances.

We recommended that the LCE require the City Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers and schedules for Cash Advances to Disbursing Officers/Special Disbursing Officers to facilitate monitoring and reconciliation with the Treasury Unit.

We recommended that Management require strict compliance to prescribed rules and procedures on the submission by the contractor of a Construction Safety and Health Program duly approved by DOLE which shall form part of the contract.

Also, for the Head of the Agency to ensure the strict compliance to Section 3.1 of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 by requiring the concerned department/offices/units to appropriately inform the SA or ATL of the notification to the public relative to the its PPAs.

We recommended that Management require all concerned to streamline the procurement/acquisition process in order to expedite the conduct of procurement activities of the City. Further, to strictly observe the applicable rules and regulations on the procurement, receipt, acceptance and issuance of goods in compliance with the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 and the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Accountant in collaboration with the City Treasurer to:

Reconcile the employee’s records with the PhilHealth and HDMF Offices to trace the affected employees on the said accumulated balances;

Check the available records to determine whether all Personal Share deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share were remitted; and

Cause the immediate remittance to PhilHealth and HDMF for the unremitted premiums/contributions and loan repayments of employees, as warranted by the result of inquiry and reconciliation with the said agencies.

We recommended that the Management: (a) reinforce the involvement and participation of barangays through massive information dissemination of the City’s solid waste management programs especially on the proper disposal of waste; and (b) support the establishment of fully functional MRF for each barangay or cluster barangay and in the meantime, provide appropriate trash bins with proper labels on assigned collection areas.

Further, we recommended the strict monitoring of areas where: (a) indiscriminate dumping of waste is rampant; and (b) burning of waste is being practiced and enforce all regulations to the extent of imposing penalties for non-compliance.

Furthermore, we recommended that the City hasten the construction and proper utilization of the City MRF, complete rehabilitation of the closed dump site at Barangay San Antonio and to fully implement all the solid waste management projects, programs and activities to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

We recommended that Management:

Develop priority and achievable programs/projects that shall cater to the immediate needs of the target beneficiaries to avoid various unimplemented GAD programs; and 

Stop the practice of including the payment of honorarium of CHW/BHW, CDW and BNS and other expenses that are not chargeable to the GAD Budget in compliance with the regulations stipulated in the JMC 2016-01.
We recommended that Management facilitate the full implementation of all BuB programs/projects/activities in order not to delay further the benefits these could provide the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management strengthen the control policies and procedures of cash collections by instructing the City Treasurer and Liquidating Officer to check and verify the Report of Collections and Deposits being submitted by Collectors to prevent errors and minimize wrongful acts, if not totally eliminated.

We also recommended that proper supervision be made by the City Treasurer to all her staff to help ensure that internal control objectives are achieved.

We likewise reiterated prior year’s audit recommendation on the preparation of Bank Reconciliation, that the City Accountant exert extra effort in gathering data/documents for them to identify/analyze/verify reconciling items, particularly those that are indicative of possible fraud.

Lastly, for the Management to continue the investigation of the uncovered cash discrepancy perpetrated by certain individual/s, and file a criminal and administrative case against those persons found liable in the said fraud.

Management was advised to transfer the ₱8.36 million to the City’s current account maintained for Trust Funds and hasten the implementation of projects/programs/activities for which such funds were created.

We also reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the ₱12.72 million investments in time deposit returned to the Special Education Fund current account. Thereafter, the Local School Board should facilitate the establishment of definite plans for its use and immediate implementation.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials and employees to utilize the Fiesta Fund raised out of donations/contributions received from other LGUs, civic organizations and private individuals in disbursing/paying for the expenses of fiesta/festival celebration/activities, before authorizing the use of other funds, if any.
Require all the 16 payees who actually received the honoraria payments, to immediately refund the amount they received, to the Office of the City Treasurer for lack of valid and legal basis

We recommended that based on the documents, the City Treasurer shall record the receipt and issuance in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.  Separate registries shall be maintained for each kind of donated goods.

We recommended that the local government shall make a supreme effort to dispose its unserviceable property and equipment in accordance with Section 125 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Vol. I, to obtain better re-sale value and/or prevent further deterioration of the assets due to long exposure to elements.

Observe promptness in the preparation and approval of procurement documents from Purchase Request to Request For Quotations until it reaches the Purchase Order to avoid unnecessary and improve process efficiency.
